name: French Sables
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: BAKERS' DOZEN (BETH SETRAKIAN) SHOW #BD1A08
1. Cream the butter and sugar together until smooth.
2. Add the egg yolk and vanilla extract, then beat thoroughly.
3. In a separate bowl, combine the flour and salt.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the butter mixture, blending until the dough is smooth.
5. Shape the dough into cylinders about 1 to 2 inches in diameter on a lightly floured surface.
6. Wrap the dough tightly in waxed paper or plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least an hour.
7. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
8. Brush the chilled logs with beaten egg.
9. Roll each log in chopped crystalline sugar.
10. Using a sharp knife, cut the dough into rounds about 1/3-inch thick.
11. Place the cookies about 1 inch apart on a parchment-covered baking sheet.
12. Bake for approximately 10 minutes, or until the edges are lightly colored.
Yield: Approximately 4 dozen Sables
